source.zh: 《希伯来圣经·创世记》1–11 章（马所拉文本；中文引文参照和合本），并参《七十士译本》创世记；后世解释参《所罗门智训》2:24、《启示录》12:9、《以诺一书》守望者书卷、《禧年书》
source.en: Genesis 1–11 in the Hebrew Bible (Masoretic Text), with the Septuagint Genesis for comparison; later interpretive layers from Wisdom of Solomon 2:24, Revelation 12:9, the Book of the Watchers in 1 Enoch, and Jubilees

note.zh:
《创世记》1–11 章在正典里是连续的一条线，但文本内部有明显的接缝。创1:1–2:4a 与创2:4b–3 是两个创造叙事：前者（学界通称祭司典 P）的顺序是植物、动物、最后一并造出男和女，称神为「神」（Elohim），六日一日一格，句式整齐；后者（耶和华典 J）先造男人，再种园子，再造走兽飞鸟带来给他起名，最后才用肋骨造女人，称神为「耶和华神」（YHWH Elohim）。犹太教与基督教的传统读法把两章视为同一次创造的概述与细述；文献假说（自 19 世纪威尔豪森以来）判其为不同来源的并置，这仍是学界主流的分析框架，但分层与年代争议不断。洪水叙事里接缝更露：动物是「每样两个，一公一母」（创6:19–20）还是「洁净的畜类七公七母」（创7:2）；雨下了四十昼夜（创7:12），而水势浩大又「共一百五十天」（创7:24）——经文把两组数字并排放着，没有调和。

创1:1 的读法本身有分歧：传统译作「起初，神创造天地」；另一读法把它当作时间状语从句（「神开始创造天地的时候，地是空虚混沌……」），中世纪注释家拉希已如此读，NJPS、NRSV 等现代译本采用。希伯来原文没有明说「从无造有」，creatio ex nihilo 是后起的神学表述。

创3 里的引诱者，经文只称「蛇」（nachash），说它比田野一切的活物更狡猾，没有说它是天使或撒但；把蛇等同于撒但，是后起的解释（《所罗门智训》2:24 把死亡归于魔鬼的嫉妒，《启示录》12:9 直接称撒但为「古蛇」），「路西法」之名则出自《以赛亚书》14:12 拉丁译本对巴比伦王的讽刺诗，被后世移用。拿非利人在正典里只占创6:1–4 这四节，名字只出现在其中一节，经文也没有明说他们就是神的儿子们与人的女子所生的那批人；《以诺一书》《禧年书》才把这几节扩写成守望者下凡、巨人横行的长篇。创1:21 神造的「大鱼」原文作 tanninim（大海兽），与诗篇、以赛亚书里的利维坦是同一族语汇，但在创1 里它们只是受造之物，没有对抗情节。挪亚洪水与两河的洪水叙事（《阿特拉哈西斯》《吉尔伽美什》第十一块泥板）在造船、放鸟、山上搁浅、事后献祭上高度重合，两者的关系是学界长期讨论的问题。

本时间线只沿《创世记》1–11 章一条线走。同属亚伯拉罕传统的伊斯兰创造叙事分散在《古兰经》多章，情节并不相同：六日创造，但明言真主不觉疲倦（50:38），因而没有第七日安息；阿丹由泥土造成，天使奉命向他叩拜，易卜劣厮拒绝；他妻子的名字不见于《古兰经》，肋骨造人之说出自圣训；阿丹与妻子一同吃了禁果、一同受责，阿丹随后得到宽恕，没有原罪的教义；努哈的洪水见于第11、71 章；巴别塔则没有对应的叙事。
note.en:
Genesis 1–11 reads as one continuous line in the canon, but the seams inside it are visible. Genesis 1:1–2:4a and 2:4b–3 are two creation accounts. The first (conventionally assigned to the Priestly source, P) runs plants, animals, then male and female together at the end, calls God Elohim, and marches through six days in fixed formulas. The second (the Yahwist, J) makes the man first, then plants the garden, then forms beasts and birds and brings them for naming, and only at the end builds the woman from a rib; it calls God YHWH Elohim. Jewish and Christian tradition has long read the two chapters as a summary followed by a close-up of the same event. The documentary hypothesis, since Wellhausen in the nineteenth century, reads them as separate sources set side by side; that remains the mainstream analytical frame, though the layering and dating are still argued over. The Flood shows the seam even more plainly: animals go in two by two (6:19–20) or seven pairs of every clean kind (7:2); the rain lasts forty days and nights (7:12), and the waters also prevail for a hundred and fifty days (7:24). The text lays both sets of numbers down without reconciling them.

Even the first verse is disputed. Traditionally it is rendered 'In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth'; it can also be read as a temporal clause — 'When God began to create heaven and earth, the earth was formless and void' — which is how Rashi took it in the eleventh century and how NJPS and NRSV translate it. The Hebrew does not say that God made the world out of nothing; creatio ex nihilo is a later theological formulation.

The tempter in Genesis 3 is only 'the serpent' (nachash), the subtlest of the wild creatures. The text does not call it an angel or Satan; that identification is later (Wisdom of Solomon 2:24 blames death on the devil's envy; Revelation 12:9 calls Satan 'that old serpent' outright), and the name Lucifer comes from the Latin of Isaiah 14:12, a taunt-song against the king of Babylon, borrowed afterwards. The Nephilim occupy four verses in the canon (6:1–4), are named in only one of them, and are never quite said to be the children the sons of God fathered; it is 1 Enoch and Jubilees that expand those verses into the long story of the fallen Watchers and their giant offspring. The 'great sea creatures' God makes on the fifth day are tanninim, the same vocabulary used of Leviathan in Psalms and Isaiah, but in Genesis 1 they are simply among the made things, with no battle attached. Noah's Flood overlaps closely with the Mesopotamian flood stories (Atrahasis, Gilgamesh XI) in the boat, the released birds, the mountain landing and the sacrifice afterwards; how the two are related has been debated for well over a century.

This timeline follows Genesis 1–11 only. The Islamic account, from the same Abrahamic stream, is scattered across the Qur'an and differs in substance: creation in six days, but with the explicit statement that no fatigue touched God (50:38), so there is no seventh-day rest; Adam formed from clay, the angels ordered to bow to him and Iblis refusing; his wife is never named in the Qur'an, and the rib comes from hadith rather than scripture; both eat the forbidden fruit, both are held responsible, and Adam is then forgiven, with no doctrine of original sin; Nuh's flood is told in suras 11 and 71; and there is no Tower of Babel.

title.zh: 地是空虚混沌，渊面黑暗
title.en: Formless and void, darkness on the deep

text.zh: 起初，神创造天地。地是空虚混沌（希伯来文 tohu wa-bohu），渊面黑暗，神的灵运行在水面上。经文开篇时水已经在那里：一片没有形状、没有边界的深渊，光还没有出现。神说要有光，就有了光。他看光是好的，把光和暗分开，称光为昼，称暗为夜。有晚上，有早晨，这是头一日。
text.en: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. The earth was formless and empty — tohu wa-bohu — darkness lay on the face of the deep, and the spirit of God moved over the waters. Water is already there on the first line; where it came from is not said. God says, Let there be light, and there is light. He sees that the light is good, divides it from the darkness, calls the light Day and the darkness Night. There is evening and there is morning: one day.

title.zh: 穹苍分开上下的水，旱地露出来
title.en: A vault between the waters, and dry ground

text.zh: 第二日，神造穹苍（希伯来文 rāqîaʿ，本义是锤打展开的薄片；《七十士译本》译作 stereōma「坚固之物」，拉丁译本沿之作 firmamentum），把穹苍以下的水和穹苍以上的水分开，称穹苍为天。第三日，天下的水聚在一处，旱地露出来；神称旱地为地，称水的聚处为海。同一日，地就发生青草、结种子的菜蔬、结果子的树木，各从其类，果子里都包着核。
text.en: On the second day God makes the raqia — a word for something hammered out flat, which the Septuagint turns into stereoma, 'a solid thing', and the Latin Bible into firmamentum — and with it holds the waters above apart from the waters below; the vault he calls Heaven. On the third day the waters under heaven gather into one place so that dry ground appears: the ground he calls Earth, the gathered waters Seas. That same day the land puts out grass, seed-bearing plants, and fruit trees with their seed inside them, each after its kind.

title.en: God speaks, and it is so

text.zh: 第四日，神造两个大光挂在穹苍上，大的管昼，小的管夜，又造众星；它们的职分是分昼夜、作记号、定节令、日子和年岁。第五日，水里滋生有生命之物，神先造大鱼（原文 tanninim，大海兽），再造各样水族与飞鸟，赐福让它们生养众多，充满海水和天空。第六日，地生出牲畜、昆虫、野兽，各从其类。每一步都以「神说」起头，以「神看着是好的」收束。
text.en: On the fourth day God hangs two great lights in the vault, the greater to govern the day and the lesser the night, and the stars with them; their office is to divide day from night and to fix signs, seasons, days and years. On the fifth the waters teem with living things — the tanninim, the great sea creatures, are named first — and birds fly across the face of the vault; both are blessed to multiply. On the sixth the land brings forth cattle, creeping things and wild animals, each after its kind. Every step opens with God speaking and closes with God seeing that it is good.

title.zh: 照着神的形象造男造女，第七日安息
title.en: Male and female in the image, then rest

text.zh: 第六日将尽，神说：我们要照着我们的形象、按着我们的样式造人，使他们管理海里的鱼、空中的鸟、地上的牲畜和一切爬行的活物。神就照着自己的形象造男造女，赐福给他们，吩咐生养众多、遍满地面、治理这地；并把结种子的菜蔬和树上的果子指派为人与走兽的食物（创1:29–30，此时还没有准许吃肉）。第七日，神歇了他一切的工，赐福给第七日，定为圣日。
text.en: Late on the sixth day God says: let us make man in our image, after our likeness, to rule the fish of the sea, the birds of the air, the cattle and everything that creeps. He makes them male and female together, blesses them, and tells them to be fruitful, fill the earth and subdue it. Seed-bearing plants and tree fruit are assigned as food for people and animals alike (1:29–30); meat is not yet allowed. On the seventh day he rests from all his work, blesses that day and sets it apart.

title.zh: 尘土造亚当，肋骨造夏娃
title.en: Dust for the man, a rib for the woman

text.zh: 创2 换一个角度重讲一遍。耶和华神用地上的尘土造人（adam 与「土地」adamah 同源），把生气吹进他的鼻孔，他就成了有灵的活人。神在东方的伊甸立了一个园子，把人安置在里面修理看守；园里长着各样悦人眼目、好作食物的树，当中有生命树和分别善恶树。一道河从伊甸流出滋润园子，分为四道：比逊、基训、希底结（底格里斯）、伯拉（幼发拉底）。神吩咐：各样树上的果子都可以随意吃，只是分别善恶树上的不可吃。神说那人独居不好，先用土造出走兽飞鸟带到他面前让他起名，仍没有合适的配偶；于是使他沉睡，取下他的一条肋骨，造成一个女人。
text.en: Genesis 2 tells it again from another angle. The LORD God shapes the man out of the dust of the ground — adam from adamah — and breathes the breath of life into his nostrils, and he becomes a living soul. God plants a garden eastward in Eden and puts the man there to work it and keep it. Trees grow there that are pleasant to look at and good for food, and in the middle stand the tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil. A river runs out of Eden to water the garden and divides into four heads: Pishon, Gihon, Hiddekel (the Tigris) and Perath (the Euphrates). Every tree is permitted but one. Saying it is not good for the man to be alone, God forms the beasts and birds from the ground and brings them to be named, and no fit companion is found among them; so he puts the man into a deep sleep, takes a rib, and builds it into a woman.

title.zh: 蛇、果子、火焰的剑，和田间的第一桩血
title.en: The serpent, the fruit, the flaming sword, and blood in the field

text.zh: 蛇比田野一切的活物更狡猾。它对女人说，吃了那果子眼睛就明亮，能像神一样知道善恶。女人见那树的果子好作食物，也悦人眼目，就摘下来吃了，又给她丈夫。二人眼睛明亮，发觉自己赤身露体，拿无花果树的叶子编裙子遮身。天起了凉风，耶和华神在园中行走，追问之后逐一宣判：蛇用肚子行走，终身吃土；女人怀胎生产必多受苦楚；地为人的缘故受咒诅，长出荆棘和蒺藜，人必汗流满面才得糊口，直到归回尘土。神用皮子给他们做衣服，把人赶出园子，在伊甸东边安设基路伯和四面转动发火焰的剑，把守通往生命树的路。园外，长子该隐种地，次子亚伯牧羊；耶和华看中亚伯献的羊群头生的和脂油，看不中该隐献的地里出产，该隐就在田间打死兄弟。血从地里发声哀告，该隐流离飘荡，带着神给他的记号，住在伊甸东边的挪得之地。
text.en: The serpent is subtler than any beast of the field. It tells the woman that the fruit will open her eyes and make her know good and evil as God does. She sees the tree is good for food and pleasant to the eyes, takes some and eats, and gives some to her husband. Their eyes open; they know they are naked and sew fig leaves together. God, walking in the garden in the cool of the day, questions them and then sentences each in turn: the serpent to go on its belly and eat dust; the woman to bear children in pain; the ground to be cursed so that it yields thorns and thistles, and the man to sweat for his bread until he returns to the dust he was taken from. God makes them coats of skin, drives them out, and sets cherubim east of Eden with a flaming sword turning every way, to guard the road to the tree of life. Outside the garden their first son Cain works the soil and the second, Abel, keeps sheep. The LORD accepts Abel's firstlings and their fat and does not accept Cain's produce, and Cain kills his brother in the field. The blood cries out from the ground, and Cain goes to live in Nod, east of Eden, marked so that whoever finds him will not kill him.

title.zh: 大渊的泉源裂开，天上的窗户敞开
title.en: The deep breaks open and heaven's windows are loosed

text.zh: 人在地上多起来，神的儿子们看见人的女子美貌，就随意挑选娶来为妻。那时候地上有拿非利人（和合本译作「伟人」）；神的儿子们与人的女子所生的，是上古英武有名的人。耶和华见人的罪恶极大，终日所思想的尽都是恶，就后悔造人，定意用水除灭。惟有挪亚蒙恩。他按吩咐用歌斐木造方舟，里外抹上松香，长三百肘、宽五十肘、高三十肘，分上中下三层，门开在旁边，带妻子、三个儿子和儿妇并各样活物进去。大渊的泉源裂开，天上的窗户敞开，雨下了四十昼夜，水势比最高的山还高过十五肘。方舟停在亚拉腊山上；挪亚先放乌鸦，再放鸽子，鸽子第二次回来时嘴里叼着一枝新拧下来的橄榄叶。出方舟后他筑坛献祭，神应许不再用洪水灭地，把虹放在云彩里作记号，并第一次准人吃肉，只是不可吃带血的肉。
text.en: As people multiply, the sons of God see that the daughters of men are fair and take them as wives. The Nephilim are on the earth in those days, and the children born of those marriages are the heroes of old, men of renown. The LORD sees that human wickedness is great and that every thought of the heart is only evil, regrets having made mankind, and resolves to blot it out with water. Noah alone finds favour. He builds the ark of gopher wood, pitched inside and out, three hundred cubits long, fifty wide and thirty high, with three decks and a door in the side, and goes in with his wife, his three sons and their wives, and the animals. The fountains of the great deep burst open, the windows of heaven are loosed, rain falls forty days and forty nights, and the water stands fifteen cubits above the highest mountains. The ark grounds on the mountains of Ararat. Noah sends out a raven, then a dove, and the second time the dove comes back with a freshly plucked olive leaf. He builds an altar and sacrifices; God promises never again to destroy the earth by flood, sets the rainbow in the cloud as the sign of it, and for the first time permits meat, though not with its blood in it.

title.zh: 塔顶通天，口音变乱，人分散全地
title.en: A tower to heaven, a confusion of speech

text.zh: 洪水之后，挪亚三个儿子闪、含、雅弗的后裔分地而居，各随各的方言、宗族立国（创10 的列国表，后世传统数为七十族）。那时天下人的口音言语还是一样的。人往东迁移，在示拿地的平原住下，学会做砖、把砖烧透，拿砖当石头，拿石漆当灰泥，动手建一座城和一座塔，塔顶通天，「为要传扬我们的名，免得我们分散在全地上」。耶和华降临察看那城和塔，变乱他们的口音，使他们彼此言语不通，工就停了，人从那里分散到全地——那城名叫巴别。族谱随后收窄到闪的后代他拉：他带着儿子亚伯兰、孙子罗得、儿妇撒莱离开迦勒底的吾珥，本要往迦南地去，走到哈兰就住下了。《创世记》的创世部分到此为止，往下是族长叙事。
text.en: After the Flood the sons of Noah — Shem, Ham and Japheth — spread out, each clan with its own tongue and territory: the Table of Nations in Genesis 10, traditionally counted as seventy peoples. The whole earth still has one language. Moving east, people settle on a plain in Shinar, learn to bake bricks hard, use brick for stone and bitumen for mortar, and start building a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, to make a name for themselves so that they will not be scattered. The LORD comes down to look at the city and the tower, confuses their speech so that no one understands his neighbour, and the work stops; from there they are scattered over the face of the earth, and the place is called Babel. The genealogy then narrows to Terah, of Shem's line, who leaves Ur of the Chaldees with his son Abram, his grandson Lot and his daughter-in-law Sarai, meaning to go to Canaan, and settles at Haran. The primeval history ends there; the patriarchal narrative begins.
